data.title: Drug Use Surveillance of Takecab Tablets for "Gastric Ulcer, Duodenal Ulcer, and Reflux Esophagitis"
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this survey is to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of vonoprazan tablets in patients with gastric ulcer, duodenal ulcer, and reflux esophagitis in the routine clinical setting.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

\- Participants with gastric ulcer, duodenal ulcer, and reflux esophagitis

Exclusion Criteria:

* Participants with previous history of hypersensitivity to ingredients in Takecab tablets
* Participants taking atazanavir sulfate or rilpivirine hydrochloride
* Participants meetin
endpoints: Percentage of Participants With Gastric Ulcer Who Had One or More Adverse Drug Reactions
Percentage of Participants With Duodenal Ulcer Who Had One or More Adverse Drug Reactions
Percentage of Participants With Reflux Esophagitis Who Had One or More Adverse Drug Reactions

data.title: Vonoprazan Study of Investigating the Effect on Sleep Disturbance Associated With Reflux Esophagitis- Exploratory Evaluation (VISTAEXE)
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to exploratorily evaluate the effect of vonoprazan 20 mg on sleep disturbance of patients with reflux esophagitis, who have heartburn and/or regurgitation and ≥ 6.0 in the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) global score despite the maintenance treatment with PPI other than vonoprazan.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Participants who completed the initial treatment with PPIs (esomeprazole, omeprazole, rabeprazole, or lansoprazole) and have received high dose PPIs (esomeprazole 20 mg, omeprazole 20 mg, rabeprazole 10 mg, or lansoprazole 30 mg) for more than 8 weeks at the time of informed
endpoints: Changes From Start of Administration (Week 0) in Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) Global Score at End of Study
